comment_text.sn1.14:1.1: Where the setting is omitted, it is assumed that a deity speaks and the Buddha answers.
comment_text.sn1.14:2.3: Sussusati, literally “wanting to listen” is a precursor for wisdom (Snp 1.10:11.3), and is normally used of mendicants who want to learn the Dhamma (MN 122:26.4).

translation_text.sn1.14:1.1: “An aristocrat is the best of bipeds,
translation_text.sn1.14:1.2: an ox is the best of quadrupeds,
translation_text.sn1.14:1.3: a maiden is the best of wives,
translation_text.sn1.14:1.4: and a first-born the best of sons.”
translation_text.sn1.14:2.1: “A Buddha is the best of bipeds,
translation_text.sn1.14:2.2: a thoroughbred, the best of quadrupeds,
translation_text.sn1.14:2.3: a good listener is the best of wives,
translation_text.sn1.14:2.4: and the best of sons is loyal.”

variant_text.sn1.14:1.2: balībaddo → balivaddo (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed); balibaddho (sya-all, km); balibaddo (mr)

reference_text.sn1.14:1.1: cck15.8, csp1ed12.7, csp2ed12.7, dr15.9, ms12S1_49, msdiv14, pts-vp-pli2ed1.13, sya15.10
